non_discriminating_assertions: All standard structural assertions (Goal, Phases, Decisions, Key Questions, Errors, Current Phase) pass for both variants on evals 1-3
Key Questions section carries over open questions (passes in both conditions for all evals)
highest_signal_assertions: Phase 1 described as minimal setup (0/4 old_skill on eval 4, 1/4 with_skill)
Phase 2 described as end-to-end vertical slice (0/4 old_skill on eval 4, 1/4 with_skill)
User feedback or sign-off after Phase 2 (0/4 old_skill on eval 4, 1/4 with_skill)
qualitative_observations: New skill produces tracer-bullet phase structure for complex software (eval 4): Phase 1 scaffolding, Phase 2 E2E slice with sign-off gate
Old skill produces architecture-first Phase 1 for complex software — heavy design phase before any implementation
Both variants produce identical structural quality for standard evals (1-3) — the tracer bullets addition is purely additive
New skill also produced findings.md and progress.md reference files per skill guidelines; old skill only produced task_plan.md
